prompt: The tour bus breaks down three hours from the next city. Show starts in five hours.

answers.label: Hire emergency vans and split the gear

outcomes.description: You make it with an hour to spare. Exhausted but the show goes on.
One van gets a flat too. You arrive with ten minutes to setup.

answers.label: Call the venue and push the start time back

outcomes.description: The venue agrees. Fans are annoyed but the show happens.
The venue can't hold the crowd that long. Half the audience leaves.

answers.label: Livestream an acoustic set from the roadside as an apology

outcomes.description: The clip goes viral. The hashtag trends for a day.
The stream drops halfway through. Fans see a pixelated mess.

prompt: A regional radio station invites your artist for a live on-air interview before the show.

outcomes.description: The interview is smooth. Ticket sales for tonight spike.
The host asks a loaded question. It gets tense.

answers.label: Decline — preserve energy for the show

outcomes.description: Smart call. Your artist is sharp and focused tonight.

prompt: A major national act is playing a venue two blocks away on the same night. Foot traffic is split.

outcomes.description: The lower price pulls in a bigger crowd than expected.
People still go to the big show. You've cut prices for a half-empty room.

answers.label: Lean into being the underdog — market it that way

outcomes.description: The underdog framing resonates. You get a surge of contrarian fans.
Nobody takes the bait. The room stays quiet.

answers.label: Start late to catch the overflow crowd after the other gig ends

outcomes.description: Smart play. A wave of people floods in after the big show wraps.
Most people go home after the big show. You played to almost nobody.
